name: cramfsswap
title: cramfsswap
summary: swap endianness of a cram filesystem (cramfs)
description:
swap endianness of a cram filesystem (cramfs)
cramfs is a highly compressed and size optimized Linux filesystem which is
mainly used for embedded applications. The problem with cramfs is that it
is endianness sensitive, meaning you can't mount a cramfs for a big endian
target on a little endian machine and vice versa. This is often especially
a problem in the development phase.
.
cramfsswap solves that problem by allowing you to swap to endianness of a
cramfs filesystem.

commands.name: cramfsswap
commands.description: swap endianness of a cram filesystem (cramfs)

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use cramfsswap because swap endianness of a cram filesystem (cramfs).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use cramfsswap as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cramfsswap <infile> <outfile>` as documented upstream for cramfsswap.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair cramfsswap with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `cramfsswap --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
